recalls.Description:
JJGoo has issued a recall for its LED balloon lights due to a serious risk of injury or death from battery ingestion. These submersible lights, sold in packs of 100, feature multi-color blinking capabilities and measure approximately 0.6 inches in diameter. Each light contains 200 preinstalled LR41 batteries, and the packaging is labeled "MY1005E-Colorfu1-100." The design allows easy access to the button cell batteries, posing a significant ingestion hazard for children.

The recalled lights do not comply with safety standards mandated for consumer products containing button cell batteries, as they lack necessary warnings under Reese's Law. Ingesting these batteries can lead to severe injuries, chemical burns, or even fatal outcomes. Consumers are urged to take immediate action to ensure safety.
recalls.Title: Recall of JJGoo LED Balloon Lights Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact JJGoo via email at JJGooLEDBalloonLightsrecall@outlook.com.

Images.Caption: The recalled JJGoo balloon lights were sold in packs of 100 LED color-changing lights
Each of the multi-color recalled JJGoo balloon lights has 200 preinstalled LR41 batteries
"MY1005E-Colorfu1-100" is printed on a label on the product packaging of the recalled JJGoo LED balloon lights

Retailers.Name: Online at Amazon.com from May 2025 through November 2025 for about $23.

Distributors.Name: Shenzhen Yimu Technology Co., Ltd. dba JJGoo, of China

Hazards.Name: Risk of ingestion due to accessible button cell batteries

Remedies.Name: Stop using the lights, keep them away from children, and contact JJGoo for a refund

seo.metaDescription: JJGoo recalls LED balloon lights due to serious battery ingestion risks. Immediate action required for consumer safety.
seo.ogTitle: Recall of JJGoo LED Balloon Lights Due to Battery Ingestion Hazard

recalls.Description: Primark has issued a recall for their Water Balloon Pumps, available in blue and pink colors. These pumps resemble a water bottle with a spray nozzle. The product code 5252801 can be found on the price tag attached to the back of the pump bottle.
recalls.Title: Primark Water Balloon Pumps Recalled Due to Injury Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For further assistance, contact Primark at 617-946-3236 between 8 a.m. and 5 p.m. ET, Monday through Friday. Alternatively, visit www.primark.com/recall or navigate to www.primark.com and select 'Recall' at the bottom of the page. You can also fill out the Primark Contact Us form for inquiries or to request a callback from Customer Service.

Injuries.Name: The firm has received two reports in the United States (and nine reports internationally) of the water balloon pumps rupturing, resulting in two reports of cuts on users' hands and fingers in the United States (and nine reports of cuts internationally).

Retailers.Name: Primark stores in the northeast and in Florida, Maryland and Illinois from March 2023 through July 2023 for about $6.

Importers.Name: Primark US Corp., of Boston, Massachusetts

Hazards.Name: Risk of physical injury due to excessive pressure causing product rupture

Remedies.Name: Return to Primark for a full refund in original payment method

seo.metaDescription: Primark recalls Water Balloon Pumps due to injury risks. Stop using immediately and return for a full refund.
seo.ogTitle: Primark Water Balloon Pumps Recalled Due to Injury Risk
